video:video.video:title: Nine people injured in Russian attack on Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: Nine people were injured, including three children, in a Russian strike on Ukraine’s second largest city, Kharkiv, on Thursday, according to Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 7
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 7th: Court releases Jeffery Epstein note; Iran says it’s reviewing latest proposal to end war; Secretary of State Marco Rubio to meet with the Pope; Hantavirus patients evacuated from cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: ASEAN leaders' contingency plan upholding international law, sovereignty and freedom of navigation
video:video.video:description: Southeast Asian leaders plan to issue a contingency plan that upholds international law, sovereignty and freedom of navigation, according to a draft declaration seen Thursday by The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Rubio arrives in Vatican ahead of audience with Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio opened a fence-mending visit to the Vatican on Thursday after President Donald Trump’s broadsides against Pope Leo XIV and the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish joined by Nat Wolff at premiere
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish describes her relationship with older songs, as she joins Nat Wolff, brother Finneas and co-director James Cameron at the premiere her new tour documentary.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo expects visit from Rubio as Trump continues to throw barbs at Pontiff
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet with Pope Leo on Thursday. The visit was first seen as an attempt to improve relations with the Trump administration following a series of attacks by U.

video:video.video:title: Memorial stones mark former homes of Holocaust victims in Berlin
video:video.video:description: German artist Gunter Demnig has installed small Holocaust memorial stones in Berlin for 30 years. These plaques are called Stolpersteine or "stumbling blocks."

video:video.video:title: Visiting US senators meet China's foreign minister in Beijing
video:video.video:description: China’s top diplomat on Thursday met with a US senate delegation visiting Beijing before President Donald Trumps’ trip to the country. (AP video shot by: Olivia Zhang)

video:video.video:title: A humanoid robot becomes Buddhist monk in South Korea ahead of Buddha's birthday
video:video.video:description: A humanoid robot in South Korea joined the Buddhist faith on Wednesday, during an ordination ceremony at Jogye Temple, located in central Seoul.

video:video.video:title: Wounded veterans snorkel with a whale shark at the Georgia Aquarium
video:video.video:description: The Georgia Aquarium invited injured veterans from the Wounded Warrior Project to snorkel with a whale shark and manta rays as part of their Military Salute Program.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief 'in touch with ship's captain regularly' amid hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus thanked the operator of the MV Hondius cruise ship for its cooperation amid the hantavirus outbreak on board.

video:video.video:title: US and Bahrain call on Security Council to pass resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The draft resolution demands Iran halt attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, stop imposing tolls, and disclose the placement of all mines to allow freedom of navigation.

video:video.video:title: South Korean Buddhist sect welcomes humanoid robot
video:video.video:description: A humanoid robot in South Korea joined the Buddhist faith on Wednesday, during an ordination ceremony at Jogye Temple, located in central Seoul.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Tennessee interrupt legislative session on redistricting
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Nashville, Tennessee, interrupted a legislative session on Wednesday as Republican lawmakers are poised to vote on a plan to reshape a majority-Black congressional district.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of volunteers build dozens of homes in a week in Atlanta with Habitat for Humanity
video:video.video:description: Over five days, nearly 2,200 volunteers with the international nonprofit will finish building 24 new housing units in Atlanta along with homeowners.

video:video.video:title: Rubio meets Pope Leo on mission to ease tensions after Trump's criticism over Iran
video:video.video:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Thursday. His visit was expected to be a fence-mending mission after weeks of tensions over the war in Iran in which US President Donald Trump has repeatedly bashed Pope Leo over his promotion of peace in the region.

video:video.video:title: Iran calls US proposal to the UN on Strait of Hormuz 'flawed' and 'politically motivated'
video:video.video:description: The resolution -- put forth by the U.S. and Bahrain -- is the second attempt to punish Iran for leveraging its hold over the vital waterway since U.S. and Israeli strikes ignited the war in late February.

video:video.video:title: People protest Tennessee’s new US House map that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis
video:video.video:description: The new voting districts signed into law Thursday by Republican Gov. Bill Lee gives the GOP a chance to win all nine of the state’s congressional seats in the November midterm elections.

video:video.video:title: Congressman promises to sue over Tennessee's new US House map
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen and the NAACP Memphis Branch are promising to fight the new U.S. House map that carves up Memphis' majority-Black congressional district and was approved by Republican lawmakers on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: How AP-NORC polls track presidential approval and key issues
video:video.video:description: Since its founding in 2011, The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research has produced hundreds of polls on timely issues, including the approval of U.S. presidents and their handling of the economy, immigration and foreign policy.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, Residents in southwest Mississippi reeling after tornadoes hit; Tennessee lawmakers pass redistricting legislation; Global health authorities scramble to contain hantavirus outbreak; Rubio meets with Pope Leo to mend relations.

video:video.video:title: Investigators searching home connected to killer of California college student Kristin Smart
video:video.video:description: Authorities entered the second day of their search Thursday at a home connected to the man convicted of killing 19-year-old college student Kristin Smart in 1996, according to law enforcement.

video:video.video:title: Former Ohio deputy found guilty of reckless homicide in the shooting of Casey Goodson Jr.
video:video.video:description: Jurors in Ohio convicted a former sheriff’s deputy of reckless homicide in the shooting of a Black man who was carrying sandwiches into his grandmother’s home.

video:video.video:title: Ships in Strait of Hormuz remain stranded amid hopes of US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of ships and thousands of mariners are still stranded at the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran controls the route, causing disruption to the global economy.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend school groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am.

video:video.video:title: Judge unseals purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note
video:video.video:description: A note that Jeffrey Epstein 's former cellmate claims to have found after the financier's first suspected jail suicide attempt in 2019 has been made public.

video:video.video:title: Tiny kitten rescued from rubble after Mississippi tornado
video:video.video:description: Storm chaser Ashton Lemley was helping search through a Mississippi trailer park destroyed by tornadoes when he found a kitten trapped inside the remains of a wall.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Lionel Richie on 'the power of dreaming'
video:video.video:description: Lionel Richie reflects on his musical legacy: "That's the power of dreaming."

video:video.video:title: Man gets life in prison for firebombing Colorado demonstration
video:video.video:description: A man was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole after pleading guilty Thursday to killing one person and injuring a dozen others in a 2025 firebombing attack on a demonstration in Boulder, Colorado, in support of Israeli hostages in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Walking through the first-ever bookless bookstore pop-up
video:video.video:description: Audible transforms the traditional bookstore into an immersive listening experience, where visitors discover stories through sound instead of flipping pages. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Russia is ramping up its attempts to kill opponents in Europe, intelligence officials say
video:video.video:description: While Russian officials have long been accused of silencing the country’s enemies abroad, three Western intelligence officials from different countries told AP that a campaign of targeted killings has ramped up since President Vladimir Putin’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: With new album on the way, Shania Twain hosts ACM Awards | AP interview
video:video.video:description: With an album on the way, Shania Twain is hosting the Academy of Country Music Awards in Las Vegas on May 17.

video:video.video:title: Rubio meets Pope Leo on mission to ease tensions after Trump's criticism over Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed “efforts to achieve a durable peace in the Middle East” in talks at the Vatican on Thursday aimed at easing tensions following U.S.

video:video.video:title: "Seductive and accessible," Himid's work brightens the British Pavilion at the Venice Biennale.
video:video.video:description: Questions about identity, migration and belonging take centre stage at the Venice Biennale's British Pavilion, where artist Lubaina Himid is representing the United Kingdom with a major exhibition of new paintings.

video:video.video:title: Henry Moore’s monumental sculptures take over Kew Gardens in London
video:video.video:description: The largest ever outdoor exhibition of Henry Moore’s sculptures is opening at London’s Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew.

video:video.video:title: A fan-run club pushes back against aggressive stadium culture in Poland
video:video.video:description: A grassroots Warsaw soccer club formed by fans more than a decade ago to resist aggressive nationalist stadium culture is hoping to find new relevance in Poland — a country whose president doesn’t deny his own past participation in fights between football fans.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes cut across Mississippi as severe storms damage homes
video:video.video:description: Powerful storms that spawned at least three tornadoes tore through several Mississippi counties. Authorities say the storms damaged nearly 500 homes, knocked down trees and power lines, and injured at least 17 people.

video:video.video:title: Trump plans dramatic makeover for Eisenhower office building
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's proposal to put a coat of white paint on the exterior of a 19th-century historic landmark building next to the White House is slated for a hearing Thursday by a key federal agency he expects to approve what would be a dramatic makeover.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in UK local elections seen as a verdict on Keir Starmer's leadership
video:video.video:description: British voters cast ballots Thursday in local and regional elections that could shake up the country’s politics and deliver a heavy blow to embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Decorated skeletons in Catholic churches in Bavaria take visitors by surprise
video:video.video:description: It is a sight that has sent shivers down the spines of many visitors: four complete skeletons draped in silk and brocade, adorned with precious stones, filigree gold, silver and lace that have been on display for centuries at the Catholic monastery church of Banz in southern Germany.

video:video.video:title: Sudan was already at war and hungry. Now its farmers are being hit by another conflict
video:video.video:description: Farmers already stressed by the war at home between Sudan's military and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces are now watching costs rise due to war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Damage at a mobile home park after powerful storms tear through several Mississippi counties
video:video.video:description: Authorities say powerful storms, that spawned at least three tornadoes, tore through several Mississippi counties, damaging nearly 500 homes and injuring at least 17 people.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants peace deal to end war in the Middle East 'more than I do'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump told reporters in Washington that the ceasefire with Iran was holding. Trump also said Iran "trifled with us," after the U.S. military said it intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz and “targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for atta

video:video.video:title: Iranian media say country's forces exchanged fire with 'the enemy' on island in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iranian state media said Thursday that the country’s armed forces exchanged fire with “enemy units” on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Leading Dutch epidemiologist on the hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A leading Dutch epidemiologist stressed Thursday that the hantavirus outbreak is "extremely unlikely" to become a pandemic.

video:video.video:title: ASEAN leaders meet for summit under pressure to mitigate backlash from war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Southeast Asian leaders expressed "serious concern" over the war in Iran and called for a "cessation of hostilities" as they met in Philippines on Friday for an annual summit.

video:video.video:title: BTS members greet thousands of fans from balcony of Mexico's National Palace
video:video.video:description: BTS members greet thousands of fans from balcony of Mexico's National Palace

video:video.video:title: How Billie Eilish breathes new life into old songs
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish describes her evolving relationship with her older songs, as she joins her co-director James Cameron at the Los Angeles launch of "Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)" (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar meets Meloni in Rome
video:video.video:description: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar met his Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni in Rome on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: World Health Organization expert says hantavirus is not the next COVID
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not like the COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Protesters rally at Alabama Senate as lawmakers weigh redistricting legislation
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a hallway outside the Alabama state Senate in Montgomery on Wednesday. Legislation authorizing special Congressional primaries moved on to the Alabama Senate, after the state House passed it.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: South Carolina redistricting proposal moves to Senate after House approval
video:video.video:description: The South Carolina House on Wednesday approved a resolution giving lawmakers permission to return later, after their regular work ends, to redraw congressional districts that could eliminate the state’s only Democratic-held district.

video:video.video:title: Naming of new species among scores of tributes honoring David Attenborough's 100th birthday
video:video.video:description: A newly discovered parasitic wasp from Chile has been named after Sir David Attenborough ahead of his 100th birthday, one of several tributes marking the broadcaster’s influence on natural history, conservation and environmental awareness.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano eruption kills at least 3 hikers, 17 injured
video:video.video:description: Mount Dukono erupted in Indonesia’s North Maluku province, killing three hikers, including two Singaporean nationals and one Indonesian, authorities said Friday, as the volcano continued to spew ash high into the air and prompted renewed safety warnings.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo to spend first anniversary of his election as pope in Naples
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is spending the first anniversary of his election as Pope in Naples, where he'll pray at a sanctuary devoted to the Madonna.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 8th: UAE reports missile and drone attack; Trump says Iran ceasefire is holding; Man sentenced to life in prison for firebomb attack on demonstrators in Colorado; Tennessee Republicans enact new Congressional districts.

video:video.video:title: Casey Goodson Jr.'s family gets 'closure' following Jason Meade reckless homicide verdict
video:video.video:description: Community members and family of Casey Goodson Jr. react to ex-deputy Jason Meade being found guilty of reckless homicide in Goodson’s killing.

video:video.video:title: At least five people killed and 38 injured in mall fire in northwestern Mexico
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and another 38 injured on Thursday after a fire broke out at a shopping mall in the Mexican city of Los Mochis, state authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after first fatality
video:video.video:description: Health officials in several countries are trying to identify and follow people who may have been exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship. (Production by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Amazon's deforestation drop meets a tougher foe: forest degradation racing ahead
video:video.video:description: Brazil is on track to record its lowest Amazon deforestation rates since 2012. Despite this, experts warn that forest degradation, driven by wildfires, logging, and drought, continues to outpace deforestation.

video:video.video:title: Oil tanker arrives in South Korea after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April
video:video.video:description: A South Korean refinery says a tanker carrying 1 million barrels of crude oil arrived off South Korea’s west coast after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Amy Grant reflects on resisting labels people put on her
video:video.video:description: In the nearly 50 years she's been working as a musician, Amy Grant has repeatedly resisted the labels others have sought to put on her.

video:video.video:title: Star Wars' Grogu is now a movie star
video:video.video:description: Star Wars' Grogu is ready to take over the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:title: Are fish and chips under threat in the UK?
video:video.video:description: For years, Brockley’s Rock in southeast London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront.

video:video.video:title: What did Ian McKellen find when he Googled himself?
video:video.video:description: As she promotes new drama "The Christophers," British screen icon Sir Ian McKellen reveals the furry film role he first found when he Googled himself. (May 8)

video:video.video:title: In the footsteps of a legend: a visit to the first field where Messi played
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi's football journey began at age five on the right wing, not for tactics but to keep him near his grandmother in case he cried.

video:video.video:title: IDF releases video of what it says is an operation to dismantle Gaza tunnel routes
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Friday that its troops had “dismantled eight underground tunnel routes” spanning more than 2.5 kilometers that were used by Hamas in the southern Gaza Strip.

video:video.video:title: Kneecap sampled court case crowds on new record
video:video.video:description: How Kneecap member Mo Chara's recent court case helped to inspire a song on the Irish rappers' second album, "Fenian."

video:video.video:title: US Secretary of State comments on efforts over Iran war, relations with the Vatican
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US is expecting a response from Iran on Friday as diplomatic discussions continue.

video:video.video:title: Hungary's incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar prepares for his swearing in on Saturday
video:video.video:description: Péter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister on Saturday, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule.

video:video.video:title: AP explains the strange outbreak of hantavirus on a cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Despite a cruise ship outbreak of a rare rodent-borne illness, global health officials say the risk to the general public remains low because hantavirus germs do not easily spread between people.

video:video.video:title: Amid confrontation with the US, an Iranian museum exhibits anti-war art by American artists
video:video.video:description: While Tehran's streets had lined with anti-American billboards and posters amid the tension in the Middle East, Iranians turned out to visit an anti-war exhibition at one of the top museums.

video:video.video:title: Starmer comments after partial results show losses for Labour, wins for Reform UK in local elections
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would carry on as prime minister despite his Labour party suffering disastrous local election losses.

video:video.video:title: Three Australian women back from Syria face slavery and terrorism charges for alleged IS group links
video:video.video:description: Three Australian women were charged on Friday with slavery and terrorism offenses after they arrived home from Syria with another 10 others whom police allege are linked to the Islamic State group.

video:video.video:title: Evidence points to human remains at home linked to Kristin Smart’s killing, sheriff says
video:video.video:description: Authorities in California say evidence suggests human remains were present at a home connected to the man convicted of killing Kristin Smart, a 19-year-old college student who went missing in 1996.

video:video.video:title: What’s in the Pentagon’s newly released UFO files?
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has begun releasing new UFO files under President Donald Trump’s push for “maximum transparency,” including videos, photos and reports of unexplained sightings from around the world.

video:video.video:title: First batch of UFO files is released as Trump urges the public to draw its own conclusions
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has begun releasing new files on UFOs, saying members of the public can draw their own conclusions on “unidentified anomalous phenomena."

video:video.video:title: Access to abortion pills under fire
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court this week restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a lower-court ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: AP explains upcoming Supreme Court battle over telehealth abortion pill access
video:video.video:description: A federal appeals court last week imposed new restrictions on access to the abortion pill mifepristone when it ruled against mail-order deliveries nationally.

video:video.video:title: Specialised tour at Berlin Zoo brings joy to people living with dementia and their carers
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Berlin is giving specialised tours for people living with dementia, which is bringing them and their carers joy.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Oakland high school that produced black MLB greats invests in new players
video:video.video:description: The Oakland high school that produced Black MLB greats Frank Robinson, Curt Flood and Vada Pinson is determined to keep baseball alive — and its new head coach Jamey Smart is ready to help.

video:video.video:title: USPS considers allowing people to ship handguns through the mail
video:video.video:description: Handguns could soon be mailed through the United States Postal Service for the first time in nearly 100 years if a proposed rule under the Trump administration takes effect.

video:video.video:title: Handwriting on newly released note matches one found after Jeffrey Epstein's his death, expert says
video:video.video:description: A note that a former cellmate said he discovered after Jeffrey Epstein's first suspected jail suicide attempt was penned by the same person as a note that authorities found in the millionaire sex offender's cell after he killed himself three weeks later, handwriting expert says.

video:video.video:title: Port Authority in New York tests drone deliveries between Manhattan and Brooklyn
video:video.video:description: The trial targets a year-end goal of delivering medical supplies, and eventually expanding to other routes. (AP video by Emily Wang Fujiyama)
video:video.video:tag: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

video:video.video:title: US health officials detail plans for American passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: The U.S. agreed to send a plane to repatriate and quarantine about 17 Americans who are still on the ship who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for three-day ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for three-day ceasefire
